channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Senate Republicans introduce bill to reform birthright citizenship, following Trump’s controversial order
item.description: Following Trump’s executive order to end birthright citizenship for the children of illegal immigrants, several Republicans introduced a bill that would reform U.S. law to accomplish exactly that.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Closing up shop at USAID
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Stacey Abrams-founded voter activist group hit with mass layoffs after record-breaking ethics fine
item.description: A nonprofit political activist group founded by Georgia perennial candidate Stacey Abrams saw major layoffs this weekend after a massive fine was imposed by the state ethics commission.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration's data deletions set off 'a mad scramble,' researcher says
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Researchers are in what one described as “a mad scramble” to sort out what public data the Trump administration has deleted from government websites and electronic publications.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE and MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump doubles down on floating Canada as 51st state amid tariff dispute
item.description: President Trump on Monday reiterated his desire to see Canada become part of the United States as he prepared to impose steep tariffs on Canadian goods.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Block on Trump administration federal funding freeze will likely be extended, Washington judge says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A judge in the nation's capital will likely keep a temporary block on a Trump administration plan for a freeze on federal funding after lawyers for nonprofit groups said they're still struggling to get promised grants and loans. U.S. District Judge Loren L.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Secretary of State Rubio confirms becoming acting USAID chief
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said he is now acting director of the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) while on a trip to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Jersey governor houses migrant at his house, tells feds 'good luck' trying to get her
item.description: New Jersey Gov. Phil Murphy is hinting that he is housing an illegal immigrant at his home, telling the feds "good luck" trying to get her.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump admin ends deportation protections for massive number of Venezuelans amid illegal immigration crackdown
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security is ending Temporary Protected Status for more than 300,000 Venezuelans in the United States already, Fox News Digital has confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump, Mexican president agree to delay tariffs for one month
item.description: President Trump and Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said they have agreed to delay tariffs by at least one month, following a call between the two leaders Monday. The 25 percent tariffs on Mexico were to be implemented Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Secretary of Defense Hegseth to visit Fort Bliss, southern border
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (KTSM) -- U.S. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth will visit Fort Bliss and the southern border on Monday, Feb. 3, Fort Bliss announced. Hegseth will visit Joint Task Force North, which is headquartered at Fort Bliss. This organization is part of U.S. Northern Command.
item.dc:creator: Dave Burge, NewsNation

item.title: Trump agrees to pause tariffs on Mexico, but import taxes still in place for Canada and China
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump held off on his tariff threats against Mexico for one month of further negotiations after Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum agreed to send 10,000 members of her country’s national guard to the border to address drug trafficking.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: WHO chief asks countries to push DC to reconsider withdrawal
item.description: For 2024-2025, the U.S. is WHO’s biggest donor by far, putting in an estimated $988 million, roughly 14% of WHO’s $6.9 billion budget.
item.dc:creator: MARIA CHENG and JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump defends tariffs as Mexico, Canada and China plan retaliation
item.description: Some of the top imported products from the United States include cars, produce, alcohol, cellphones, clothes and shoes.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: Temporary protection for about 350,000 Venezuelans to end: DHS
item.description: Noem revoked one of two Temporary Protected Status designations for Venezuela, the New York Times first reported.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Trump used fentanyl to justify tariffs, but the crisis was already easing
item.description: The White House says fentanyl smuggled to the U.S. justifies tariffs against Canada, China and Mexico. But fentanyl deaths and smuggling have been dropping fast — and Canada plays almost no role.
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann, MPR News

item.title: Trump's second week in office delivers jolts and chaotic orders with a mix of politics and tragedy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's second week in office seemed to deliver a daily dose of deliberate jolts for the country. There were chaotic reminders of his first term. The White House found itself backtracking on its directive to freeze federal spending on grants and loans.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, CHRIS MEGERIAN and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump wants to undo diversity programs. Some agencies react by scrubbing US history and culture
item.description: TUSKEGEE, Ala. (AP) — The tails of the Alabama Air National Guard’s F-35 Lightnings are painted red, like those of the Guard's F-16s before them. It's an homage to the famed Alabama-based unit of the Tuskegee Airmen, who flew red-tailed P-51 Mustangs during World War II.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER and GARY FIELDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio visiting Central America with the Panama Canal and immigration top of mind
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Secretary of State Marco Rubio is on his first foreign trip in office, arriving in Central America on Saturday to press President Donald Trump's top priority — curbing illegal immigration — and to bring the message that the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he ordered airstrikes on Islamic State in Somalia
item.description: "These killers, who we found hiding in caves, threatened the United States and our Allies," Trump said in a post on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: Weekend vote to decide if Ken Martin jumps from Minnesota Democratic perch to national party head
item.description: Members of the Democratic National Committee are due to vote Saturday on who will lead the party. After the 2024 election, Democrats are regrouping.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: More migrants deported under Biden, Baja state official says
item.description: Tijuana Mayor Ismael Burgueño Ruiz says he will not lift an emergency declaration he made last month as a way to prepare for mass deportations from the United States.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5

item.title: President Trump casts blame during briefing on deadly plane crash
item.description: President Donald Trump started Thursday’s press briefing with a moment of silence for the victims and their families after a deadly crash involving an American Airlines plane and an Army helicopter near Ronald Reagan National Airport on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, Fox 5

item.title: Former Congresswoman Shelley Berkley settles in her new role as Las Vegas mayor
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- Mayor Shelley Berkley is settling into her new role after decades of Goodman leadership in Las Vegas. The former congresswoman now holds the title of mayor.
item.dc:creator: John Langeler, KLAS, CBS News
